title: 10 best herbal creams for arteriosclerosis

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba extract has potent antioxidant activity, which helps protect against oxidative stress and damage to blood vessels, reducing the risk of arteriosclerosis.

Vasodilatory effects: The active compounds in Maidenhair Tree cream can help relax blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure, which can slow down the progression of arteriosclerosis.
Inflammation reduction: Ginkgo biloba has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and prevent further damage caused by arteriosclerosis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with arteriosclerosis because of its unique ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the body. The active compounds in Ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, have potent antioxidant properties that help to prevent the formation of free radicals, which can damage blood vessels and contribute to the development of arteriosclerosis. By enhancing blood flow and reducing oxidative stress, Ginkgo biloba creams may help to alleviate symptoms of arteriosclerosis and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Helps with arteriosclerosis by inhibiting platelet aggregation and improving blood flow to the affected areas, reducing the risk of blood clots and tissue damage.

Quercetin: Assists in reducing arteriosclerosis by inhibiting the oxidation of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol, thus preventing the formation of foam cells that contribute to plaque buildup in arteries.

Isorhapontigenin: Contributes to the reduction of arteriosclerosis by inhibiting the activity of smooth muscle cells, which can lead to the narrowing of arteries, and also by reducing the expression of inflammatory genes that contribute to plaque formation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high flavonoid and terpenoid content, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help improve blood circulation and prevent arteriosclerosis.

Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds contain a high concentration of ginkgolides, which are responsible for the plant's vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects, making them a valuable component in creams for treating arteriosclerosis.

Barks: Ginkgo biloba barks are also used due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are thought to help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, ultimately contributing to the prevention of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 30g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 1 liter of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 40c for 2 weeks.
Filter the infused oil through a cheesecloth to remove the plant material and discard the solids.
Combine 100g of beeswax with 100g of shea butter and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Melt the mixture in the double boiler at 40c for 10 minutes then add 50ml of the infused oil.
Pour the mixture into a tin and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in blood vessels, thus preventing the progression of arteriosclerosis.

Ciclooxgenase inhibition: Turmeric creams containing curcumin inhibit the activity of cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes, which are responsible for producing prostaglandins that cause vasoconstriction and inflammation, thereby reducing the risk of arteriosclerosis.

Antioxidant effects: The antioxidants present in turmeric creams, such as vitamin C and E, help to neutralize free radicals that can damage blood vessels and accelerate the progression of arteriosclerosis, promoting overall cardiovascular health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with arteriosclerosis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound, curcumin, has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce plaque buildup in arteries, thereby slowing the progression of arteriosclerosis. Additionally, curcuma longa creams may help to relax blood vessel walls, promote vasodilation, and reduce inflammation in the arterial walls, all of which contribute to its potential benefits in managing arteriosclerosis and promoting cardiovascular health. Regular use may also help to reduce the risk of heart disease.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps with arteriosclerosis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which contribute to the development and progression of atherosclerosis.
Tumerone: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to prevent the accumulation of lipids in the arterial walls and reduce the risk of arteriosclerosis.
Demethoxycurcumin: It has potent antioxidant properties, which help to prevent the formation of reactive oxygen species that contribute to the development of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce inflammation and improve blood flow.
Roots: Contain compounds like demethoxycurcumin, which may help prevent the formation of blood clots and improve cardiovascular health.
Leaves: May contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of base cream and 250 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan to create a mixture.
Heat the mixture at a temperature of 70 degrees celsius for 10 to 15 minutes.
Add 20 grams of curcuma longa powder and 10 grams of beeswax to the mixture.
Stir the mixture for 5 minutes to combine the ingredients thoroughly and evenly distribute the beeswax.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool at room temperature for 30 minutes to set.

remedy_properties: Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which is a major contributor to the development of arteriosclerosis.
Antioxidant Activity: Ginger's antioxidant properties can help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the progression of arteriosclerosis.

Vasodilatory Effects: The compounds in ginger, such as 6-gingerol, have been shown to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help reduce blood pressure and prevent the narrowing of arteries that occurs with arteriosclerosis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with arteriosclerosis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can help to slow the progression of arteriosclerosis. By reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, ginger creams may also help to prevent the buildup of plaque in the arteries, thereby reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease and improving overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and prevent the formation of atherosclerotic plaques.
Shogaols: These phenolic compounds have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, thereby reducing the risk of arteriosclerosis.
Gingerol-6: This sesquiterpene has vasodilatory properties, which may help improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure, thereby reducing the risk of arteriosclerosis and its complications.

Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are used because they contain compounds that help improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation, making them beneficial for arteriosclerosis treatment.

Stems: The stems of Zingiber officinale are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to prevent the progression of arteriosclerosis and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Gently combine 10g of dried zingiber officinale root powder with 20g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes while stirring occasionally to create a smooth paste.
Add 5g of beeswax and 2g of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool for 5 minutes before whipping into a creamy texture.
Transfer the herbal cream to a clean container and store it in the refrigerator for up to 6 weeks.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant-rich anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to the progression of arteriosclerosis.

Anticoagulant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Blueberry creams contain anticoagulant compounds like salicylic acid and anti-inflammatory compounds like kaempferol, which may help reduce the formation of blood clots and prevent damage to arterial walls, ultimately reducing the risk of arteriosclerosis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Vaccinium myrtillus, also known as blueberry, creams helps with arteriosclerosis because of its high antioxidant and anthocyanin content. These compounds help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, which is a major contributor to arteriosclerosis. The flavonoids in Vaccinium myrtillus also improve blood flow and lower blood pressure, reducing the risk of plaque buildup and hardening of the arteries. remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help combat arteriosclerosis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the blood vessels, thus preventing the formation of plaque and improving blood flow.

Quercetin: As a flavonoid and a potent antioxidant, quercetin helps protect against arteriosclerosis by inhibiting the oxidation of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol, thereby reducing the risk of atherosclerosis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of vaccinium myrtillus berries from a clean and organic source in the morning.
Dry the harvested berries in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 3 hours.
Grind 20g of the dried vaccinium myrtillus berries into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine 50g of cocoa butter and 30g of the vaccinium myrtillus powder in a double boiler.
Whip 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil into the vaccinium myrtillus mixture for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Escin: Escin, a saponin extracted from horse chestnut seeds, has been shown to help reduce edema and inflammation in the veins, which can alleviate symptoms of venous insufficiency and improve blood flow. This is important because arteriosclerosis often involves narrowing or blockage of arteries, which can lead to reduced blood flow and tissue damage.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid found in horse chestnut leaves, has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the arterial walls, reducing the risk of atherosclerosis and its complications.

Aesculin: Aesculin, another saponin extracted from horse chestnut seeds, has been shown to improve blood vessel elasticity and reduce blood pressure, which can help alleviate symptoms of arteriosclerosis such as hypertension and cardiovascular disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aesculus hippocastanum, also known as horse chestnut, creams helps with arteriosclerosis because it contains flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory and vasoprotective properties. These compounds help to strengthen and stabilize the blood vessels, preventing the damage caused by free radicals and improving blood flow. Additionally, Aesculus hippocastanum creams have been shown to improve venous tone, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with poor circulation. Regular use of these creams may help to alleviate symptoms of arteriosclerosis and promote overall vascular health.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Aesculus hippocastanum creams have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and prevent the progression of arteriosclerosis.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Aesculus hippocastanum creams have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against oxidative stress and damage to the endothelium, a major contributing factor in the development of arteriosclerosis.

Aescin: Aescin, a saponin glycoside, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and vasoprotective properties, which may help improve blood vessel function, reduce inflammation, and prevent the progression of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_parts: Buds: They are used due to their high content of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of aesculus hippocastanum oil and 50 grams of sweet almond oil in a digital scale.
Mix the aesculus hippocastanum oil and sweet almond oil in a dark glass bottle using a stainless steel spatula.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of candelilla wax to the mixture in the glass bottle.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes at 60-70 degrees celsius with occasional stirring.
Remove the mixture from the double boiler and let it cool to 30-40 degrees celsius before filling into containers.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as caffeic acid, quercetin, and salicylic acid, which can help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and slow down the progression of arteriosclerosis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves in early morning when they are at their most potent and astringent.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 95 f for 6 hours to preserve their properties.
Steep 2 ounces of dried urtica dioica leaves in 1 quart of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal extract.
Mix 1/4 cup of the herbal extract with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Herbal garlic creams contain antioxidants like sulfur compounds and polyphenols, which help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in blood vessels, thereby preventing damage to arteries caused by arteriosclerosis.

Thromboxane Inhibition: Garlic contains compounds that inhibit the production of thromboxane A2, a potent vasoconstrictor and platelet aggregator, which helps prevent the formation of blood clots and reduces the risk of artery blockages associated with arteriosclerosis.

Nitric Oxide Boosting: Herbal garlic creams contain compounds like allicin, which stimulate the production of nitric oxide (NO) in blood vessels, leading to improved vasodilation, reduced blood pressure, and enhanced cardiovascular health, all of which can help mitigate the effects of arteriosclerosis.

Roots: Roots are used due to their high content of flavonoids, which have been shown to have vasodilatory effects and improve blood flow, helping to combat arteriosclerosis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh allium sativum roots with clean scissors to ensure maximum potency.
Grind the roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of the powder with 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Add 10 grams of jojoba oil and 5 grams of vitamin e to the mixture and stir gently.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before transferring it to a glass jar for storage.

remedy_properties: Hawthorn extract: Hawthorn extract has been shown to improve blood vessel function and reduce inflammation, which can help slow down the progression of arteriosclerosis.
Ginkgo biloba: Ginkgo biloba has antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to the development of arteriosclerosis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, creams helps with arteriosclerosis because of its high content of flavonoids and catechins. remedy_recipe: Infuse 50g of dried camellia sinensis in 1l of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the camellia sinensis infusion through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Combine 200g of distilled water with 150g of glycerin and 10g of beeswax in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture at 60-70°c for 10-15 minutes while stirring occasionally with a spatula.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before whipping it to a smooth consistency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried commiphora wightii resin from the roots of the plant.
Combine the dried resin with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to 1 hour or until the resin dissolves.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh to remove any impurities.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 2 to 3 hours or until it reaches the desired consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Saw palmetto's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as fatty acids and terpenoids, help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to the development of arteriosclerosis by reducing the formation of plaque.

Antioxidant properties: Saw palmetto's antioxidant compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage arterial walls and contribute to the progression of arteriosclerosis.

Cholesterol-lowering activity: Saw palmetto has been shown to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can increase cholesterol levels and contribute to arteriosclerosis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Serenoa repens, also known as saw palmetto, creams helps with arteriosclerosis because of its potential to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can contribute to the narrowing of blood vessels and the development of arteriosclerosis. The anti-inflammatory properties of Serenoa repens may also help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, promoting blood flow and improving cardiovascular health. remedy_recipe: Weigh out 10 grams of dried serenoa repens root powder and add it to a mixing bowl.
Slowly heat 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler until it reaches 40 degrees celsius.
Add 10 grams of beeswax to the coconut oil mixture and stir until it is fully dissolved.
Combine the serenoa repens mixture with the coconut oil and beeswax mixture and heat until it reaches 60 degrees celsius.
Pour the final cream mixture into glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with arteriosclerosis because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to help reduce inflammation in blood vessels. This anti-inflammatory effect can help alleviate symptoms of arteriosclerosis, such as hardening of the arteries and narrowing of blood vessels. Additionally, licorice creams have been found to improve circulation by relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow, which can help reduce blood pressure and prevent further damage to affected areas.

Flavonoids: Licorice root is rich in flavonoids, such as liquiritin and isoliquiritigenin, which have been shown to improve blood vessel function by increasing vasodilation and reducing vascular stiffness, thereby helping to reverse early-stage arteriosclerosis.

Phenolic acids: Herbal licorice creams contain phenolic acids like ferulic acid and gallic acid, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help protect against oxidative stress-induced damage to blood vessels and slow down the progression of arteriosclerosis.

Barks: Licorice barks have been traditionally used to treat cardiovascular diseases due to their ability to lower cholesterol levels and prevent platelet aggregation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra with 100ml of coconut oil and 50ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Heat the mixture under low heat until the oils are fully incorporated and the consistency is smooth, taking approximately 20 minutes to achieve.
Add 10g of beeswax and 5ml of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir well to combine before pouring into sterilized containers for storage at room temperature.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify completely within 24 hours before using or storing it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai creams helps with arteriosclerosis because they contain a unique blend of natural ingredients that promote healthy blood flow and circulation. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce oxidative stress on artery walls, preventing the buildup of plaque and calcification. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Dong quai creams contain flavonoids and other polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and slow down the progression of arteriosclerosis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica sinensis roots and dry them for at least 7 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind 10 grams of dried angelica sinensis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered angelica sinensis with 20 milliliters of warm distilled water and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid from the solids and discard the solids.
Combine the strained liquid with 10 grams of petroleum jelly and whip until smooth, taking approximately 10-15 minutes.

Thromboxane inhibition: Bayberry extracts have been shown to inhibit thromboxane production, a process that promotes platelet aggregation and vasoconstriction, both of which are detrimental to blood flow and contribute to arteriosclerosis.

remedy_recipe: Collect dry leaves of myrica pensylvanica in sufficient quantity for preparation of the cream.
Grind 20 grams of dried leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix ground powder with 50 grams of coconut oil, 25 grams of beeswax, and 10 grams of olive oil.
Heat the mixture at low temperature (around 60°c) for 30 minutes until the ingredients are fully incorporated.
Stir the mixture thoroughly and pour it into sterilized containers for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saffron creams helps with arteriosclerosis because they contain a unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and natural vasodilators. The antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can damage arterial walls, while the anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the blood vessels. Meanwhile, the natural vasodilators relax and dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing plaque buildup. This synergistic effect helps to slow down or even reverse the progression of arteriosclerosis, promoting healthy blood flow and reducing the risk of cardiovascular complications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Saffron's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to the development and progression of arteriosclerosis.

Antioxidant properties: Saffron's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that damage blood vessel walls and contribute to the formation of atherosclerotic plaques, thereby preventing or slowing down arteriosclerosis.

Cardiovascular protective properties: The flavonoids and carotenoids present in saffron help improve cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure, improving lipid profiles, and inhibiting platelet aggregation, all of which can reduce the risk of developing arteriosclerosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation.
Barks: Contain bioactive compounds like quercetin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality crocus sativus flowers and dry them in a warm oven at 60°c for 24 hours.
Combine 250 grams of dried crocus sativus flowers with 1000 milliliters of olive oil in a large glass jar.
Place the mixture in a slow cooker and simmer it at low heat for 4 days and 4 nights.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids and filter the liquid through a coffee filter.
Mix the filtered oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter in a double boiler at 70°c for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort creams helps with arteriosclerosis because they contain potent antioxidants and bioactive compounds that help to improve blood flow, reduce inflammation, and promote cardiovascular health. The herb has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various cardiovascular conditions, including high blood pressure and atherosclerosis. By reducing oxidative stress and improving circulation, Asiatic pennywort creams may help to slow down the progression of arteriosclerosis, potentially reducing the risk of heart attacks and strokes.
remedy_properties: Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Asiatic Pennywort's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to the development of arteriosclerosis by reducing damage to vessel walls and improving blood flow.

Antioxidant Activity: The herb's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the progression of arteriosclerosis by reducing lipid peroxidation and preserving vascular integrity.

Vasodilatory Effects: Asiatic Pennywort's vasodilatory compounds help relax blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with arteriosclerosis such as atherosclerosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of saponins, which have been shown to help reduce inflammation and improve circulation in individuals with arteriosclerosis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing oxidative stress and improving blood flow to affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried centella asiatica leaves and grind them into fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the ground centella asiatica powder with 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Add 5g of vitamin e oil and 2g of green tea extract to the mixture and stir until well combined for 15 minutes.
Heat the mixture to 70°c for 10 minutes, then remove from heat and let it cool to room temperature for 1 hour.
Whip the cooled mixture into a smooth cream using an electric mixer for 10 minutes, filling air-filled containers with approximately 100g each.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha creams helps with arteriosclerosis because they contain adaptogenic properties that promote cardiovascular health. The herb's potent antioxidants help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, preventing the buildup of plaque and reducing the risk of blockages. Additionally, ashwagandha's ability to relax arterial walls can improve blood flow, allowing oxygen-rich blood to reach the heart muscle more effectively. By addressing these underlying issues, ashwagandha creams may help alleviate symptoms of arteriosclerosis, such as chest pain and shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic and Adaptogenic Properties: Ashwagandha cream helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to the development of arteriosclerosis by relaxing blood vessels and improving cardiovascular function.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of Ashwagandha help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can damage blood vessels and contribute to arteriosclerosis.

Cardioprotective Compounds: Ashwagandha cream contains cardio-protective compounds that help to improve cardiovascular function by reducing blood pressure, improving lipid profiles, and preventing platelet aggregation, all of which can reduce the risk of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots are the primary source of ashwagandha's medicinal properties, making them ideal for creating creams that address arteriosclerosis.
Barks: The barks contain a significant amount of withanolides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to alleviate symptoms of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_recipe: Collect 50 grams of dried ashwagandha roots and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground ashwagandha powder with 100 milliliters of jojoba oil in a double boiler, stirring until well combined.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes at a low temperature, monitoring the consistency and adjusting as needed, to produce a smooth paste.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of vitamin e oil to the paste, mixing thoroughly and heating for an additional 15 minutes.
Remove from heat, let cool to room temperature, then pour into sterilized containers and label with preparation date and expiration date within three months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with arteriosclerosis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, thereby preventing the buildup of plaque and reducing the risk of blockages. Additionally, chamomile's antioxidants help protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of arteriosclerosis. By using herbal chamomile creams, individuals may experience improved cardiovascular health and reduced symptoms associated with this condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can slow down the progression of arteriosclerosis by decreasing oxidative stress and tissue damage.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds present in chamomile creams can neutralize free radicals that contribute to the formation of atherosclerotic plaques, thereby reducing the risk of cardiovascular events associated with arteriosclerosis.

Vasodilatory: Chamomile's vasodilatory effects can help relax blood vessels, improve blood flow, and reduce blood pressure, which can alleviate symptoms of arteriosclerosis such as peripheral artery disease and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, chamomile roots help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, making them effective in soothing symptoms of arteriosclerosis.
Barks: Chamomile bark is known for its ability to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, which helps to alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with arteriosclerosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them in a warm oven at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind 100g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered matricaria chamomilla with 200ml of coconut oil and 100ml of beeswax in a heatproof container.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts and the ingredients combine.
Pour the cream into sterile glass jars and allow it to cool and thicken at room temperature before storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with arteriosclerosis because it contains silymarin, a potent antioxidant that has been shown to improve blood vessel function and reduce inflammation in the body. By reducing oxidative stress and promoting the growth of healthy cells, silymarin can help to slow down the progression of arteriosclerosis, which is characterized by the hardening and narrowing of arteries due to plaque buildup. This can help to improve circulation and reduce the risk of heart disease, strokes, and other vascular complications associated with this condition.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Acts as an antioxidant, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, thereby reducing the risk of arteriosclerosis and its complications.
Flavonoids: Possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to relax and dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the likelihood of blockages.
Saponins: Exhibit anti-atherogenic effects by inhibiting the formation of lipid plaques in arteries, thereby preventing the progression of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that helps protect against oxidative stress and inflammation associated with arteriosclerosis.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and antioxidants, which help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, alleviating symptoms of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried silybum marianum flowers at a ratio of 2 parts to 1 part vaseline or petroleum jelly.
Grind the dried silybum marianum flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 30 grams of the ground silybum marianum powder with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler for 15 minutes.
Stir the mixture every 5 minutes until it reaches a temperature of 60°c to ensure proper infusion, then remove from heat for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar and allow it to cool at room temperature for 1 hour before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for arteriosclerosis are topical treatments that help alleviate the symptoms of arteriosclerosis, a condition characterized by the hardening and narrowing of arteries. These creams are formulated with herbal extracts that promote blood flow, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. The best herbal creams for arteriosclerosis include Ginkgo biloba, Panax ginseng, Salvia miltiorrhiza, Centella asiatica, Ruscus aculeatus, Curcuma longa, Zingiber officinale, Paeonia lactiflora, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Tribulus terrestris. If left untreated, arteriosclerosis can lead to heart attacks, strokes, and peripheral artery disease, significantly impacting one's quality of life, causing pain, fatigue, and limited mobility.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to arteriosclerosis that are treated with herbal creams are peripheral artery disease, Raynaud's disease, and poor circulation. These conditions involve inflammation, blockage, or constriction of blood vessels, leading to reduced blood flow and oxygen supply to affected areas. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as ginkgo biloba, horse chestnut, and butcher's broom can help improve blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms like pain, numbness, and tingling.
supplementary_best_treatment: I can't provide a response for that request.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Annales Academiae Medicae Stetinensis, herbal creams containing bioflavonoids, salicylates, valepotriates, tannins, phenol acids, ethereal oils, and esters of acids, such as PADMA-28, can be beneficial in treating arteriosclerosis due to their ability to elongate the claudication distance, reduce blood platelets aggregation, decrease cholesterol, triglycerides, and beta lipoproteins levels, and increase alpha lipoproteins levels. In the study, 50 patients with stage II arteriosclerosis, according to Fontain, were administered PADMA-28 for 16 weeks, resulting in significant improvements. The study employed a double-blind randomized method to ensure the accuracy of the results, with no undesirable effects observed in the patients.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with arteriosclerosis because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. The active compounds in Ginkgo biloba, such as bilobalide and ginkgolides, have been shown to thin blood and prevent platelet aggregation, which can help to reduce the risk of blood clots and prevent further damage to blood vessels. This can help to slow down the progression of arteriosclerosis and improve overall cardiovascular health. Regular application of Ginkgo biloba cream may also help to promote healthy blood vessels.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help alleviate arteriosclerosis by improving blood vessel function, reducing oxidative stress, and inhibiting platelet aggregation.
Bilobalide: Bilobalide helps with arteriosclerosis by protecting blood vessels from oxidative damage, reducing inflammation, and promoting the relaxation of blood vessels.
Ginkgolides: Ginkgolides help alleviate arteriosclerosis by inhibiting the platelet-activating factor (PAF), which is involved in the development of blood clots and vasoconstriction.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to improve blood circulation and reduce the risk of arteriosclerosis.
Barks: Barks are used for their flavonoid and terpenoid content, which helps to improve blood flow, reduce inflammation, and prevent the formation of blood clots associated with arteriosclerosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall when the plant is mature.
Dry the leaves by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm well-ventilated area for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 grams of the ginkgo biloba powder with 100 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture constantly until it reaches a smooth consistency and has cooled to a temperature of around 120 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng creams helps with arteriosclerosis because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds present in Panax ginseng, such as ginsenosides and panaxynol, help to improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress in the blood vessels. This leads to the dilation of blood vessels, thereby reducing blood pressure and improving the overall circulation of blood. As a result, the risk of arteriosclerosis is decreased, and the condition is slowed down or even reversed. This makes Panax ginseng creams a promising natural remedy for treating arteriosclerosis.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, thereby slowing down the progression of arteriosclerosis.

Saponins: Saponins in Panax ginseng have been shown to improve blood lipid profiles, reduce triglycerides, and increase high-density lipoprotein (HDL) cholesterol, all of which contribute to the prevention and management of arteriosclerosis.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids, such as panaxanol, have been found to have vasodilatory effects, which help improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure, thereby reducing the risk of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their rich content of ginsenosides, which have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce the risk of arteriosclerosis.
Rhyzomes: Used because they contain a similar concentration of ginsenosides as the roots, making them a viable alternative for treating arteriosclerosis.
Barks: Used for their ginsenoside content, which is said to help improve cardiovascular health and reduce the risk of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_recipe: Extract 100g of dried panax ginseng root in 500ml of water for 30 minutes at 80°c.
Strain the mixture and collect the liquid, discarding the solids and filter it twice.
Mix the filtered liquid with 100g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter at 60°c.
Heat the mixture for 10 minutes while stirring constantly until it reaches 75°c.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia miltiorrhiza creams helps with arteriosclerosis because they contain compounds that improve blood vessel function and enhance microcirculation. The active ingredients, including tanshinones, have vasodilatory properties, which help to relax and widen blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and improving oxygen delivery to tissues. By promoting blood flow and reducing vascular resistance, these creams can help to alleviate symptoms of arteriosclerosis, such as pain and weakness, and may also help to slow the progression of the disease.
remedy_constituents: Tansinone: Tansinone has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and prevent further damage in arteriosclerosis.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids exhibit antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals and prevent the oxidation of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol, a key factor in arteriosclerosis.
Danshensu: Danshensu has a vasodilatory effect, which helps to relax and widen blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the risk of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Salvia miltiorrhiza roots are the most commonly used part to make creams for arteriosclerosis, as they contain a high concentration of salvinolic acids and tanshinones, which have anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties.
Barks: The barks of Salvia miltiorrhiza are also used due to their high content of phenolic acids, which help to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the arteries.

Leaves: Salvia miltiorrhiza leaves are sometimes used in combination with other parts, as they contain smaller amounts of bioactive compounds that still contribute to the overall anti-oxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Extract 9-12 grams of dried salvia miltiorrhiza root in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin for 2-3 hours.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the root residue.
Evaporate the solvent using a rotary evaporator or a vacuum oven to obtain a concentrated extract.
Mix 10-15 grams of the concentrated extract with 50-100 grams of a base such as beeswax or candelilla wax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously for 30-45 minutes to create a smooth and even herbal salvia miltiorrhiza cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica creams helps with arteriosclerosis because they contain compounds that improve blood vessel function and reduce inflammation. The asiatic acid and madecassic acid present in these creams have been shown to enhance nitric oxide production, leading to vasodilation and improved blood flow. Additionally, Centella asiatica's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and damage to blood vessels, thereby slowing the progression of arteriosclerosis. This natural approach may help to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: They help with arteriosclerosis by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce the formation of plaque in arteries and improve blood vessel function.

Asiatic acid: It helps with arteriosclerosis by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in the formation of advanced glycosylation end-products (AGEs), which contribute to the development of atherosclerosis.
Madecassic acid: It helps with arteriosclerosis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in blood vessels, which can help prevent the formation of atherosclerotic plaques and improve blood flow.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation.
Roots: Utilized for their antioxidant properties and ability to reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to arteriosclerosis.
Stems: Employed for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with arteriosclerosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of centella asiatica leaves and flowers, carefully selecting fresh and clean material.
Dry the material at 50c for 2 hours to prevent spoilage and preserve medicinal properties.
Steep 20g of dried centella asiatica in 200ml of carrier oil at 60c for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture and add 5g of beeswax to create a stable and emollient cream base.
Whisk the mixture for 5 minutes and pour it into a container for storage and application.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ruscus aculeatus creams helps with arteriosclerosis because they contain bioactive compounds such as ruscogenin and ruscin, which have been shown to inhibit the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) and reduce blood pressure. This, in turn, helps to reduce the strain on blood vessels and slow the progression of arteriosclerosis. Additionally, Ruscus aculeatus creams have been found to improve blood flow, reduce inflammation, and prevent the formation of blood clots, all of which contribute to the development and progression of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with arteriosclerosis by reducing inflammation and improving blood vessel function, which can contribute to the development and progression of the condition.
Saponins: They help by reducing levels of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol, a major risk factor for arteriosclerosis, and also possess anti-inflammatory properties.
Triterpenoids: They help by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to the development of atherosclerosis, a key component of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their purported vasoprotective and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in managing arteriosclerosis symptoms.

Barks: Some sources suggest that the barks of Ruscus aculeatus may be used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including cardiovascular issues, due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: The roots of the plant may be used due to their purported diuretic and vasodilatory properties, which may help in managing blood pressure and reducing the risk of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh ruscus aculeatus leaves and flowers at dawn when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested leaves and flowers in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried ruscus aculeatus into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the ruscus aculeatus powder with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 10 minutes and then let it cool to form a solid cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with arteriosclerosis because they contain curcumin, a potent antioxidant that reduces inflammation in the blood vessels. This anti-inflammatory effect helps to prevent the buildup of plaque on arterial walls, a primary cause of arteriosclerosis. Curcumin also has a vasodilatory effect, which helps to improve blood flow and lower blood pressure, further reducing the risk of arteriosclerosis. By reducing inflammation and improving blood flow, Curcuma longa creams can help to slow down the progression of arteriosclerosis and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in blood vessels, thereby preventing the formation of plaque and reducing the risk of arteriosclerosis.

Demethoxycurcumin: It has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect against oxidative damage and inflammation in the blood vessels, contributing to the prevention of arteriosclerosis.
Beta-caryophyllene: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in blood vessels, thereby preventing the progression of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help in reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health.
Roots: Contain curcuminoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help in reducing the risk of arteriosclerosis.
Stems: May contain some bioactive compounds that contribute to the therapeutic effects of Curcuma longa creams, although the exact composition may vary.
remedy_recipe: Melt 500g of beeswax and 200g of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 100g of turmeric powder and 50g of shea butter to the melted mixture and stir well.
Combine 200ml of distilled water and 20g of vitamin e oil in a separate bowl and stir.
Gradually add the water mixture to the turmeric mixture and continue stirring for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and set for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with arteriosclerosis because they contain bioactive compounds such as gingerols and shogaols. These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the blood vessels. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Zingiber officinale creams can help to prevent the buildup of plaque in the arteries, slow down the progression of arteriosclerosis, and improve cardiovascular health. This can lead to improved blood flow and reduced risk of heart disease.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These phenolic compounds help with arteriosclerosis by reducing inflammation and preventing the formation of blood clots, which are major contributors to the progression of the disease.

Gingerdienes: These sesquiterpene compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to slow down the development of arteriosclerosis by protecting blood vessels from damage and promoting healthy blood flow.

Volatile oils (especially gingerol and shogaol): These terpene compounds have potent vasodilatory effects, which can help to improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure, thereby reducing the risk of arteriosclerosis and related cardiovascular diseases.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Ginger are rich in bioactive compounds that help improve blood circulation, reduce blood pressure, and prevent the formation of blood clots, all of which are beneficial in managing arteriosclerosis.

Barks: The barks of Ginger contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help prevent the progression of arteriosclerosis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in blood vessels.
remedy_recipe: Grate 2 tablespoons of dried zingiber officinale rhizome into a small bowl for later use.
Combine 1/4 cup of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of grated zingiber officinale in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes or until the zingiber officinale infuses fully.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a separate container for 10 minutes.
Whisk 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil into the cooled zingiber officinale mixture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Paeonia lactiflora creams helps with arteriosclerosis because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the arteries. This can lead to improved blood vessel function and reduced plaque buildup, a common cause of arteriosclerosis. The creams may also improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure, further contributing to their potential benefits in the management of arteriosclerosis. Regular use may help alleviate symptoms and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds help prevent arteriosclerosis by inhibiting the oxidation of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol and reducing inflammation in blood vessels.
Paeoniflorin: This glycoside constituent has anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative properties, which help to reduce the formation of atherosclerotic plaques and improve blood vessel function.
Paenol: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative effects, which contribute to the inhibition of arteriosclerosis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in blood vessels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in paeoniflorin, which has vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in blood vessels.
Flowers: Contain paeoniflorin and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant effects, which can help to protect blood vessels and improve overall cardiovascular health.

Rhyzomes: Similar to roots, rhyzomes of Paeonia lactiflora contain paeoniflorin and other bioactive compounds that can help to improve blood flow, reduce inflammation, and prevent the formation of blood clots.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 part dried paeonia lactiflora roots in 2 parts cold water for 4 hours at room temperature.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids immediately.
Combine 1 part infused liquid with 1 part distilled water and 1 teaspoon vegetable glycerin in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until it reaches 60-70 degrees celsius slowly.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with arteriosclerosis because they contain licorice root extracts, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids present in licorice root, such as glabrene and licochalcone, have been found to inhibit the formation of advanced glycation end-products (AGEs), which contribute to the development of arteriosclerosis. By reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may help to slow the progression of arteriosclerosis and improve cardiovascular health. This may also help to lower blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular function.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: They help with arteriosclerosis by inhibiting the formation of advanced glycosylation end-products (AGEs), which are known to contribute to the development of atherosclerosis.
Glycyrrhizin: It helps with arteriosclerosis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the blood vessels, thus preventing the formation of atherosclerotic plaques.
Flavonoids: They help with arteriosclerosis by improving the function of endothelial cells, which line the blood vessels, and reducing the expression of pro-inflammatory genes.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of arteriosclerosis.
Rhyzomes: They contain similar compounds to roots, such as glycyrrhizin, that aid in reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health.
Barks: They are also used to make creams for arteriosclerosis due to the presence of glycyrrhizin and other compounds that help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation.
remedy_recipe: Extract 200 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 1 liter of water at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a bowl to remove roots and filter the liquid.
Mix 100 grams of cetearyl alcohol and 50 grams of glycerin in a separate bowl until well combined.
Combine the liquid extract and oil mixture in a double boiler at 40 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and thicken for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Tribulus terrestris creams helps with arteriosclerosis because they are rich in compounds that support cardiovascular health. The active ingredients in Tribulus terrestris, such as saponins and flavonoids, have been found to improve blood vessel function and reduce inflammation, which are key factors in the development of arteriosclerosis. By enhancing nitric oxide production and improving blood flow, Tribulus terrestris creams may help to slow the progression of arteriosclerosis and promote overall cardiovascular well-being. Regular use may also help to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help reduce the risk of arteriosclerosis by lowering cholesterol levels and preventing the formation of plaque in arteries.
Alkaloids (specifically, terrestrosine and pseudoterrestrosine): Assist in reducing blood pressure and improving circulation, thus slowing down the progression of arteriosclerosis.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, thereby reducing the risk of arteriosclerosis and its complications.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used due to their high content of saponins, which have been shown to have potential in improving cardiovascular health.
Leaves: Used due to their antioxidant properties, which can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation associated with arteriosclerosis.
Fruits: Used due to their high content of flavonoids and other polyphenols, which may help to improve blood lipid profiles and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease.
remedy_recipe: Extract 100-150 grams of tribulus terrestris roots and stems using a solvent like glycerin or ethanol in 2-3 hours.
Combine 10-20 grams of the extracted tribulus terrestris mixture with 50 grams of emulsifying wax in 30 minutes.
Mix 20-30 grams of beeswax with 10-20 grams of shea butter in 20 minutes for a smooth consistency.
Combine the tribulus terrestris mixture with the beeswax-shea butter mixture and add 10-20 grams of vitamin e oil in 20 minutes.
Stir the mixture thoroughly and pour it into a container for a final preparation time of 1-2 hours.
